svg.title: MiniNet TX versus RX per-client data structures
svg.desc: Side-by-side comparison of the TX path structures (PacketQueue linked list and outbound SharedQueue) versus the RX path structures (rx_ring circular buffer and rx_ready condvar), showing why each was chosen.
svg.text: malloc each node · grows on demand

g.text: circular array · fixed cap · no malloc

svg.text: TX uses linked list (unbounded, burst-tolerant) · RX uses ring buffer (fixed, zero-alloc, DMA-safe)
